F0.1-shadowing-launder.verdict: REJECTS at the clause v2 names
F0.1-shadowing-launder.clause: 5.1 [BRAND-1.1]: TYPE-6 no-shadowing/no-redeclaration applied verbatim to brand binders, firing at the shadowing mint binder (the parameter 'q is live; the mint binder respelling 'q is a redeclaration). OWN-3-style within-function uniqueness independently blocks it.
F0.1-shadowing-launder.variants_walked: Fresh-binder variant (mint 'p, push t_p into seq<cq_tx<'q,..>,4>): rejects at seq_push TYPE-5 argument mismatch (cq_tx<'p> != element cq_tx<'q>). Two-line return variant (mint 'p, return t_p as cq_tx<'q>): rejects at the 5.3 callee-side RETURN check, which is the ordinary TYPE-5 return-type match on the brand constituent. All three exits of the v1 forge are closed.

F0.2-stale-id-reattach.verdict: REJECTS at the clause v2 names
F0.2-stale-id-reattach.clause: 5.2 R6-shr-class freeze: id1 is live at `let parked = move a1`, and `moved` is in the freeze's enumerated list, so the move is the firing site; the same-name re-let is never reached.
F0.2-stale-id-reattach.backstop: Even if the freeze were deleted, the literal v1 program still rejects: the re-let creates a NEW binder (TYPE-6 bars only live names), and id1's identity is env(old-a1) per 5.1, so use_id's R10(a)-place/TYPE-5 comparison fails (old binder != new binder).
F0.2-stale-id-reattach.caveat: A SIBLING program (not the v1 forge) escapes as drafted: a same-binder `set ar = <new arena>` after the freeze record expires while a smuggled copy of the id survives (give/box transit) re-attaches at the SAME place with a TYPE-5 pass. Filed as the MAJOR copy/record finding; the v1 program itself is dead.

F1-same-region-two-arena.verdict: REJECTS at the clause v2 names
F1-same-region-two-arena.clause: 5.2: the brand is the resolved PLACE, not the region; a1 and a2 are distinct places in one region 'r, so `use_id(ar: &'r a1, id: id2)` fails the R10(a)-place comparison (recorded place(a2) != resolved place of the argument tied to place(ar) = a1). Types alone (both ahdl<.,u64> in 'r) no longer decide it.
F1-same-region-two-arena.variants_walked: Struct-field variant (two.a/two.b): distinct resolved places, rejects — matches the ratified ATK-two-struct-rings precedent verbatim.

F1-container-transit-launder.verdict: REJECTS at the clause v2 names
F1-container-transit-launder.clause: 5.1 container chain: seq_push of cq_tx<'b2,T> into seq<cq_tx<'b1,T>,N> is a TYPE-5 argument-type mismatch at the push ('b2 != 'b1 as nominal binder-resolved constituents). The natural pop is sound: identity re-derived from the stated element type per 5.3 CONTAINER EXTRACT, and every element in the seq was exact-matched on entry.
F1-container-transit-launder.note: Brand targs parse by riding the REGIONID targ production with sort resolved at the binder (5.1 namespace + 5.5 wall); the GRAM-2 `brand 'q : ep` binder production is used-but-unamended — filed MINOR, does not reopen the forge.

F1-vacuous-return-launder.verdict: REJECTS at the clause v2 names
F1-vacuous-return-launder.clause: 5.3 RETURN: the callee-side check is a TYPE-5 brand-constituent match on the returned value's stated type against the declared result brand — it consults no loan entry, so it fires for entry-less brand carriers. `return move b` (b: cq_tx<'r>) against result cq_tx<'p> rejects; doubly covered by the ordinary return-type match, redundant by design as claimed.

D1-factored-producer-loop.walk: Tie 5.4: one parameter carrier (tx) — legal (v1's exactly-one clause gone). Seed 5.3: tx seeded rigid 'q. Effects: brand-free per 5.5; cq_try_send exhibits writes('e) on the loop-local endpoint borrow region, confined to the body, so produce's row is spellable under EFF-1 unchanged (the v1 EFF-1 MAJOR is resolved by elimination). Loop: &uniq 'e tx re-minted per iteration with 'e loop-local satisfies OWN-11; no live loans at the back edge, R12 clean. Caller `produce<B>(tx: move t, n: 100_u64)`: TYPE-5 brand match.
D1-factored-producer-loop.gates: Honestly BRAND-MINT-gated (naming B), as the corrected adoption note states; ALSO SEALED-MATCH-gated (the caller's `match move ends` destructure rides CQ-4's open flag) — omitted from v2's gating list, filed MINOR.

D2-arena-id-helper.verdict: DOES NOT CHECK AS SPELLED — the free result region 'v breaks it in both readings (finding 2)
D2-arena-id-helper.walk: The place-brand machinery itself is sound: h ties to ar via place(ar), R10(a)-place fires at calls, the freeze permits the & receiver. But `-> &'v T` with 'v carried by no parameter: read as a user fn, the body's return-of-borrow judgment cannot order two caller-supplied regions ('a vs 'v, OWN-3 incomparability fails closed) — a wrong-reject of the delta's own derivation (MAJOR per the review instruction); read as the sealed arena_get row, the caller instantiates 'v freely and books a content borrow that outlives the arena — an accepted use-after-free (FATAL). Accepts cleanly after the one-token repair `-> &'a T` plus a stated loan column (ISSUE & content at 'a).

D3-two-endpoints-two-helpers.walk: produce<B>(tx: move txB) and consume<B>(rx: move rxB): each helper single-carrier, each 'q independently instantiated to B; B fresh-minted, assumed-distinct from any C (5.1). BRAND-MINT-gated, honestly stated.

D4-forgery-negative.walk: f[brand 'q : spsc](tx: cq_tx<'q,u64>, rx: cq_rx<'q,u64>) is now DECLARABLE (two carriers, >=1 required — the v1 wrong-reject is fixed); the call f<A>(tx: move txA, rx: move rxB) rejects at the carrier-equality check: rx argument type cq_rx<B> vs instantiated parameter cq_rx<A>, TYPE-5/R10(a) mismatch. Either instantiation choice (A or B) fails on the other carrier.

D5-mint-and-return.walk: Result-only 'q legal solely as the pack (5.4); the 5.3 RETURN check verifies the returned identity is the body's fresh mint binder; two-arm bodies returning different fresh mints are path-consistent (one executes). Unpack binder freshness is enforced by 5.1 (TYPE-6 on existential-unpack binders). BRAND-MINT-gated as stated. Gap folded into finding 1: the pack binder must carry the ep bound (spsc/mpmc) or every clone under the unpacked brand fails closed.

findings.target: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md 5.5 (ep bound) x TAG-1 — brand instantiation boundary
findings.title: The ep tag bound is declared but never checked at any brand instantiation: an spsc brand passes into a `brand 'q : mpmc` parameter and the body lawfully mints a second producer on an spsc queue
findings.detail: 5.5 gives brand parameters a bound (`brand 'q : ep`) so bodies know the discipline, and cq_tx_clone checks the BOUND — but no clause (i) records a bound on a minted brand ('qa from cq_new<u64, spsc, 4>() has no stated bound anywhere), or (ii) compares the argument brand's bound against the parameter's declared bound at a brand targ. TYPE-5 cannot catch it: ep is deliberately NOT in the endpoint type ('inherits ep from 'q's bound, so it is not repeated in the type'), so the value-argument comparison sees cq_tx<'qa,u64> vs cq_tx<'q:='qa,u64> — a match. Forge: `fn fan[brand 'q : mpmc, 'x](tx: &'x cq_tx<'q, u64>) -> own cq_tx<'q, u64> { let t2: own cq_tx<'q, u64> = cq_tx_clone(tx); return move t2; }` — clone legal under the mpmc bound. Caller: `let ends: own cq_ends<'qa, u64> = cq_new<u64, spsc, 4>(); match move ends { QueueEnds(tx: ta, rx: ra) => { region 'x { let t2: own cq_tx<'qa, u64> = fan<'qa>(tx: &'x ta); } } }` — nothing evaluates spsc-vs-mpmc at `fan<'qa>`; a compliant checker that verifies sort and identity only ACCEPTS. Result: two live tx on one spsc instance — the sealed spsc implementation's single-producer index ownership is violated (memory-unsafe inside the sealed form even single-threaded, a data race once Delta 1 lands). This is the v1 ep-transport MAJOR half-fixed: v2 added the bound, not the boundary check.
findings.minimal_fix: Two sentences in 5.5: (i) a mint binder's bound is the mint row's ep tag (`cq_new<T, spsc, K>` introduces 'q : spsc); the existential-unpack binder restates and must match the packed declaration's bound. (ii) At every brand instantiation (brand targ at a call, unpack, any brand-sorted position), the argument brand's bound tag must equal the parameter's declared bound tag — TAG-1 closed-set equality, re-checked per monomorphization for ep-tag-generic helpers per FN-2 — and a bound-free brand argument in a bounded slot, or any mismatch, rejects citing CQ-3/TAG-1.

findings.target: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md 5.2 arena_get row and derivation 2 — result region 'v
findings.title: arena_get's result region 'v is carried by no parameter: the sealed-row reading lets a caller book a content borrow at an arbitrary region that outlives the arena (accepted use-after-free); the user-fn reading wrong-rejects derivation 2
findings.detail: Row as drafted: `arena_get['a, 'v](ar: &'a arena<'a, T>, h: ahdl<place(ar), T>) -> &'v T`. 'v appears only in the result. Sealed rows are trusted from their signature; OWN-10 is consulted only where a borrow_expr is minted, which here is inside the sealed body. Forge: `fn leak['o]() -> &'o u64 reads() { region 'i { let ar: own arena<'i, u64> = arena_new<'i, u64>(...); let h: own ahdl<place(ar), u64> = arena_mint(&uniq 'i ar, 7_u64); let rv: &'o u64 = arena_get['i, 'o](ar: &'i ar, h: h); return rv; } }` — 'v := 'o (caller-supplied), the return passes OWN-4 ('o outlives-or-equals 'o), STOR-3 frees the arena on the region-exit edge, and the caller dereferences freed storage. Every kernel precedent ties a borrow result to the receiver's region (slice_of, seq_get: -> &'r T). Read instead as a user fn, the body cannot return &'v T at all: 'a and 'v are distinct caller-supplied regions, OWN-3 incomparability fails closed — derivation 2 wrong-rejects (the outcome the review instruction pre-rates MAJOR). Secondary gap in the same rows: no loan column and no effect row are stated, so nothing rejects arena_mint (&uniq receiver, may grow/realloc the slab) while a content borrow from arena_get is live.
findings.minimal_fix: Delete 'v. Spell `arena_get['a](ar: &'a arena<'a, T>, h: ahdl<place(ar), T>) -> &'a T`, loan column `ISSUE & (content) at 'a` (the seq_get precedent, so a subsequent &uniq-receiver op on the arena rejects under the live content issue), effects `reads('a), traps` (generation/liveness trap retained, as already stated). Fix derivation 2's signature identically. Give arena_mint its row columns (loan NONE, effects writes-receiver/allocates(heap), traps) and note its R15 interior-address obligation is discharged by the ISSUE clause blocking mint-under-borrow.

findings.target: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md 5.5 [AMD-6] vs RULES-RATIFIED.md R1 and the 97-program corpus
findings.title: AMD-6's word-strike of `endpoint` from R1 delists the ratified corpus's LOAN-HOLDING ring endpoints: P5 (canonical two-ring REJECT), A11 (ACCEPT), and ATK-two-struct-rings all rest on endpoint-confinement, and the amendment does not scope the strike
findings.detail: The 97-program corpus models ring endpoints as confined shr-loan tokens (programs_ast.py: tok('p','&uniq','Prod','shr',('r',)) for P5 and ATK-two-struct-rings; A11's transfer seeds (x,shr,p) per R10(c); ATK-zero-region-endpoint's rationale quotes R1 endpoint-confinement verbatim: 'the queue can never die under a live endpoint'). Striking the word `endpoint` from R1's opaque-confined list removes the license for Prod/Cons's confined declaration; since R4 loan holders must be confined, `issues Prod (shr)` then has no legal holder. An implementer regenerating the form tables faces a fork the text does not resolve: reclass Prod as a brand-carrier (but the class requires 'holds NO loan' — contradiction, so the ring form becomes undeclarable and A11, a ratified ACCEPT, wrong-rejects; re-typing Prod<'r> with a place-brand also cross-sorts A11's `transfer<'a,'b>(x: &'a ring<T>, ..., p: own Prod<'a>)` signature against the 5.5 two-sort wall), or drop the loan clause (then P5's R10(a) has no entry to compare and the canonical two-ring forgery ACCEPTS — the FATAL branch). Under the correct scoping, P5 still rejects VERBATIM by ratified R10(a) source-place comparison, clause unchanged — v2 just never says so. Also: AMD-6's claim 'endpoints were never R2-confined' misstates the ratified baseline (R1 listed them, R2 bound them); and the voided ATK-zero-region-endpoint guarantee must be re-discharged for brand-carriers via CQ-5 joint heap ownership. R2 storage reopening is otherwise intentional and defended: seq elements by the 5.1 chain, box by type-driven identity, user structs closed by the absence of a brand gparam on user declarations.
findings.minimal_fix: Scope the amendment by declaration, not by noun: 'A form-table result type is either an opaque-confined LOAN TOKEN (declares a loan clause: issues/consumes/reissues) or an affine BRAND-CARRIER (declares a brand constituent in its type, loan clause NONE); the classes are disjoint, fixed at form declaration, and R15 fails closed on a result declaring both. The strike covers only brand-carrying endpoint types (cq_tx/cq_rx); loan-issuing endpoint result types (a ring's Prod/Cons) remain opaque-confined under the token kind, so P5/A11/ATK-two-struct-rings keep their ratified verdicts and clauses.' Add one sentence: a brand-carrier's internals may reference only storage owned by the carrier set (heap, torn down by the CQ-5 endpoint-drop protocol) — never region-bound storage or borrows — restating the guarantee ATK-zero-region-endpoint's R1/R13 proof used to carry; and correct 'were never R2-confined' to 'are no longer'.

findings.target: KERNEL-DELTAS-DRAFT.md 5.2 x [AMD-6] — ahdl copy-vs-affine and freeze-record lifecycle
findings.title: ahdl is 'a copy handle out' in 5.2 but a member of AMD-6's AFFINE brand-carrier class; with copy semantics and per-id records, a copy smuggled through give/box outlives its record, the freeze expires early, and a same-binder `set`-over re-attaches a stale id with a TYPE-5 pass
findings.detail: 5.2 arena_mint: '(records ar's resolved place; a copy handle out)'. AMD-6: 'the AFFINE BRAND-CARRIER class: an affine value with full R8 transfer'. Both cannot hold. If copy: OWN-1 copies are used bare and untracked; 'freeze records expire R7-style at the ids' scope end' keys records to bindings, and a copy can outlive its binding. Forge (accepted as drafted if `box` is not read as a 'container element position' — 5.2's fail-closed names only seq): `region 'r { let ar: own arena<'r, u64> = arena_new<'r, u64>(); let b: own box<ahdl<place(ar), u64>> = match f { True() => { let h: own ahdl<place(ar), u64> = arena_mint(&uniq 'x ar, 7_u64); let bb: own box<ahdl<place(ar), u64>> = box_new(h); give move bb; } ... }; /* h's record expired at arm scope end; no live record -> freeze off */ set ar = <fresh arena of the same type>; /* 'assigned over' now legal; SAME binder, so place(ar) is unchanged */ let h2: own ahdl<place(ar), u64> = deref-copy out of b; let v: &'r u64 = arena_get(&'r ar, h2); }` — TYPE-5 passes (same binder), the stale id addresses the successor arena: generation collision at best, and if the successor is smaller, the retained generation/liveness check itself reads slot metadata out of bounds. The re-LET path stays closed (new binder); only the same-binder set-over survives, and only via record expiry. Two adjacent unpinned points: whether the freeze covers OVERLAPPING places (R6 does; v2 says only 'its recorded source place' — a struct-held arena two.a plus `set two = ...` is the same hole one level up), and whether Option/box/user-generic instantiation positions count as the fail-closed 'container element position'.
findings.minimal_fix: Pick affine (strike 'a copy handle out'; ids transfer by R8 like every carrier, matching AMD-6) — or, if copy is wanted, state type-driven records. Either way add one clause: 'a freeze record exists for every live BINDING whose stated type transitively carries the lexical-sort brand (box, Option, any generic position included), created at let/extract/result-stamp, expiring R7-style at that binding's scope end, and the freeze covers the recorded place and every overlapping place per OWN-7.' Alternatively (smallest v0 cut): broaden 5.2's fail-closed from seq to EVERY element/payload/generic-instantiation position — a lexical-sort brand may appear only at the top level of a binding, parameter, or result type — which makes per-binding records trivially complete.

findings.title: The `brand 'q : ep` binder spelling and brand targs are used without amending GRAM-2/GRAM-3, unlike TAG-1 which amends both for tags
findings.detail: GRAM-2: `region_params := "[" REGIONID ("," REGIONID)* "]"` and `gparam := TYPEID (":" TYPEID)? | "const" IDENT ":" type` — `fn produce[brand 'q : spsc]` has no production. Brand targs defensibly ride the REGIONID targ production with binder-resolved sorts (5.1 namespace + 5.5 wall), but the binder-with-bound spelling has no grammar at all. The v1 review flagged exactly this; v2 fixed the namespace and sorts but not the productions.
findings.minimal_fix: One grammar line in 5.1 or 5.5: region_params entries gain `"brand" REGIONID (":" TAG)?`; note that GRAM-3 targ's REGIONID production covers brand names, sort-checked at the binder per the 5.5 wall.

findings.title: SEALED-MATCH (CQ-4) gates derivations 1, 3, and 5 alongside BRAND-MINT, but v2's corrected gating list names only BRAND-MINT
findings.detail: Every generative derivation destructures cq_ends via `match move ends`, which rides OPEN-FLAG (SEALED-MATCH) — sealed-enum matchability and enum brand-parameterization are separate kernel-delta work per CQ-4. v2 corrected the v1 draft's false 'lands immediately' claim for BRAND-MINT but reproduces the same class of omission for SEALED-MATCH; only the arena (lexical) half truly lands immediately, which the adoption note gets right for BRAND-MINT reasons alone.
findings.minimal_fix: In open question (1) and the Delta-5 adoption bullet, write 'BRAND-MINT- and SEALED-MATCH-gated' for the generative derivations.

findings.title: Blanket 'brand-carriers are Sendable' covers the lexical sort, whose cross-thread inertness currently rests on arena having no CONC-2 capability row at all
findings.detail: A sent ahdl is unusable off-thread only because arena appears in no Sendable/Shareable row (CONC-2 fail-closed) and arena_get needs an arena borrow. That is a coincidence of the current table, not a stated rule; a future arena capability row would silently create a cross-thread stale-freeze channel (the sender's freeze records are per-function).
findings.minimal_fix: One sentence: lexical-sort carriers are Sendable only while their source form is neither Sendable nor Shareable (currently vacuous for arena); revisit with the freeze-record story before any arena capability row is added. Delta-1 review inherits this pin.

verdict: V2'S CORE HOLDS; THE DELTA DOES NOT PASS AS DRAFTED — REVISE (v2.1) AND RE-REVIEW FOUR NAMED CLAUSES, NO ARCHITECTURAL REWORK. Regression: all five v1 forge programs reject at exactly the clauses v2 names (F0.1 at 5.1/TYPE-6 mint-binder redeclaration; F0.2 at the 5.2 freeze with an independent binder-identity backstop; F1 two-arena at the 5.2/R10(a) place comparison; container transit at TYPE-5 seq_push; vacuous return at the 5.3 callee-side check). Derivations: 1, 3, 4, 5 check as claimed (the v1 tie-clause and effect-row wrong-rejects are genuinely fixed; BRAND-MINT gating is honest, SEALED-MATCH gating omitted — MINOR); derivation 2 does NOT check as spelled — its free result region 'v wrong-rejects as a user fn and, worse, as the sealed arena_get contract licenses a cross-function dangling content borrow (FATAL; one-token fix to &'a T plus a stated ISSUE clause). BRAND-INV survived every move/push/pop/match/rebind/give/nested-call attack: with identity defined as a cache of the type's brand constituent and every boundary a TYPE-5 comparison, no observable type-vs-record divergence is constructible. The two new FATALs are both at the seams v2 added: (1) the TAG-1 ep bound exists but is never checked at brand instantiation — an spsc brand into a `brand 'q : mpmc` slot lawfully clones a second producer on an spsc queue (concrete forge; two-sentence fix: mint fixes the binder's bound, instantiation compares bounds by closed-set tag equality); (2) the arena_get 'v hole above. MAJORs: AMD-6's word-strike of `endpoint` from ratified R1 delists the 97-program corpus's loan-holding ring endpoints — P5's canonical two-ring rejection and A11's ACCEPT rest on endpoint-confinement, and the amendment must scope the strike by declaration class (loan clause => confined token; brand + NONE => carrier), after which P5 still rejects verbatim under ratified R10(a); and the ahdl copy-vs-affine contradiction plus unstated freeze-record lifecycle leaves a same-binder set-over staleness path (freeze must be per-carrying-binding, overlap-inclusive, or the lexical fail-closed broadened to all element positions). All four fixes are one-to-three sentences in the existing structure.
hardest_failed: BRAND-INV cache-desync via brand-generic transit: inside f[brand 'q] pushed the rigid-'q endpoint into a body-local seq<cq_tx<'q,..>,N>, returned the seq, popped in the caller under instantiation A; also g[brand 'q1, brand 'q2] called with both instantiated to the same A (aliased rigid symbols), plus rebind/deref-box/give round-trips. DEFEATED: every identity source (let, move, push, pop, match, give, call result, seed) is defined as the stated type's brand constituent, and TYPE-5 compares that constituent at every boundary, so record and type cannot diverge observably; two rigid parameters sharing one caller instance are merely assumed-distinct — fail-closed wrong-reject at worst, never confusion.

Loop-iteration brand coexistence through an outside binding: tried moving/rebinding iteration-minted endpoints into outside-declared bindings and containers to make two dynamic instances coexist under one static symbol. DEFEATED: the mint binder is body-scoped (dies per iteration), no outer type can spell it, OWN-11 blocks moves of outside bindings into loops, and R12/AMD-4's brand-record comparison forces back-edge equality — coexistence is unspellable, confirming 5.5's freshness theorem.

Shadow-free return launder: mint a FRESH binder 'p inside a [brand 'q] helper (no TYPE-6 violation) and return the 'p endpoint as the declared cq_tx<'q> result, betting the return path only consults loan entries (empty for carriers). DEFEATED: the 5.3 callee-side RETURN check is the TYPE-5 brand-constituent match on the stated type, entry-independent, so cq_tx<'p> vs cq_tx<'q> rejects at the return statement.

Stale-id re-attach after freeze expiry via re-let (the F0.2 family pushed harder): consume all ids, move the arena, re-let the same name, present an old smuggled id. DEFEATED: a re-let is a NEW binder, and the id's identity is env(old binder), so R10(a)-place/TYPE-5 rejects regardless of freeze state — only the SAME-binder set-over path survives, which requires the copy/record gap and is filed as the MAJOR finding, not a defeat of the drafted clauses on the original forge.
